Professor View 01 / 36
Foundations for Professional English -- 30 Aulas

Gleice Leonardo Rocha de Souza

De "tô engessada na hora de falar" a fazer contato lá fora com autonomia

A1+ Santo André, SP Gerente de Excelência Comercial — Sanofi Medley 60 min / Online
Progresso Geral0%
Velocidade:
Gleice Leonardo Rocha de Souza
43 anos
Gerente de Excelência Comercial e Transformação — Sanofi Medley
Santo André, SP (Grande ABC)
A1+ (limiar A2 receptivo)
Foundations for Professional & Entrepreneurial English
30 aulas de 60 min (2x/semana)
Online (Zoom)

Jornada de Transformação

De: “Entendo quase tudo, mas na hora de falar, tô engessada — e fico dependendo dos outros para me comunicar.”

Para: “Faço contato lá fora sozinha, conduzo reuniões internacionais e represento meu negócio em inglês com confiança.”

Promessa do Programa

De “tô engessada na hora de falar” a fazer contato lá fora com autonomia — porque você já entende quase tudo. Agora é hora de destravar o que você já sabe.

Forças

  • Compreensão auditiva e leitora fortemente desenvolvida
  • Resiliência e senso de humor como ferramentas de aprendizagem
  • Histórico comprovado de progresso rápido em imersão (Berlitz Orlando)
  • Vocabulário receptivo robusto em contexto corporativo
  • Autoconhecimento pedagógico elevado

Pontos de Melhoria

  • Produção oral travada — verb tenses, pronomes e word order sob pressão
  • Consistência de prática — padrão de evolução seguida de abandono
  • Escuta de sotaques não americanos
  • Vocabulário ativo limitado para produção espontânea
  • Dificuldade em construir frases complexas em tempo real

Currículo — 30 Aulas

#TemaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalHomework
1Who Is Gleice? — Diagnostic & Self-IntroductionSubject pronouns, present simpleIntroduction role-playRecord 60s self-introduction
2My World at Sanofi — Job & TeamPresent simple 3rd person, possessivesTeam descriptionWrite 5 sentences about team
3A Day in My Life — RoutinesPresent simple, frequency adverbsRoutine orderingRecord daily routine
4Questions That Connect — Asking & AnsweringQuestion formation, Wh-questionsInformation gap role-playWrite 5 questions
5Block 1 Review — Confidence CheckAll aulas 1–4 structuresIntegrated simulationAudio reflection
6My Career Story — Where I StartedSimple past, regular verbsCareer timelineWrite career timeline
7Key Moments — Turning PointsSimple past, irregular verbsStory-tellingRecord key career moment
8The Pharma World — Industry VocabularyPresent simple + industry termsIndustry role-playMatch industry vocab
9Meetings at Sanofi — Basic Meeting LanguageCan/could, meeting phrasesMeeting simulationWrite meeting agenda
10Block 2 Review — Progress CheckAll aulas 6–9 structuresIntegrated simulationAudio reflection
11First Contact — Phone & EmailPresent continuousPhone call role-playWrite first contact email
12My Business Idea — Elevator PitchGoing to for plansPitch practiceRecord 90s pitch
13Describing Products — What We OfferAdjectives, comparativesProduct presentationWrite product description
14Polite Requests — Can, Could, WouldModal verbsRequest chain activityWrite polite email
15Block 3 Review — Confidence BoostAll aulas 11–14Networking simulationAudio reflection
16Understanding Proposals — Reading DocumentsPast simple review, business vocabDocument analysisSummarize a proposal
17I Agree, But... — OpinionsOpinion framesDebate simulationWrite opinion paragraph
18Planning Ahead — Next StepsGoing to vs willPlanning meetingWrite action plan
19Working with Partners — Cross-CulturalIndirect languagePartner meeting simCultural research
20Block 4 Review — Full SimulationAll aulas 16–19Business meeting simAudio reflection
21Pitching Your Project — Business PlanPresent simple + going toPitch presentationRecord pitch
22Brand Representation — Commercial VocabBusiness termsTrade show role-playWrite brand intro email
23Professional Emails — Formal WritingFormal email structureEmail workshopWrite proposal email
24Building Trust — Small Talk & Follow-upsPresent perfect for experienceNetworking role-playWrite follow-up
25Block 5 Review — Entrepreneurial PracticeAll aulas 21–24Partner simulationAudio reflection
26Leading a Discussion — Your VoiceTransition wordsLeading meeting role-playPrepare meeting agenda
27Handling Problems — SolutionsFirst conditionalProblem-solvingWrite solution email
28Presenting Ideas — Show and TellPresentation phrasesMini-presentationRecord 3-min presentation
29Real World — Full Business SimulationAll structures40-min immersionFinal portfolio audio
30Celebration & Next StepsReview & celebrationProgress presentationContinuation plan

Welcome, Gleice!

Onboarding

Before each class, complete the Pre-class activities below. Here are 5 emergency phrases you can use anytime:

1Could you repeat that, please?
2I do not understand. Could you explain?
3Let me think about that for a moment.
4That is a great question.
5In my experience, I would say...
Aula 01 -- Pre-class

Who Is Gleice?

Diagnostic & Self-Introduction: Subject pronouns & Present simple
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Look at each word, its definition, and the example. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Manage -- to be in charge of a team or process
"I manage a team of thirty-four people at Sanofi Medley."
Lead -- to guide or direct a group of people
"Gleice leads the Commercial Excellence department."
Represent -- to act or speak for someone or a company
"I want to represent international brands in Brazil."
Industry -- a type of business or manufacturing
"The pharmaceutical industry is very competitive."
Role -- a position or function in a company
"My role is to improve commercial processes."
Project -- a planned piece of work with a specific goal
"I have a new project to contact brands abroad."
Goal -- something you want to achieve
"My goal is to speak English with confidence."
Challenge -- something difficult that tests your abilities
"The biggest challenge is speaking in real time."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word to its definition. Take your time. Getting it wrong is part of learning.

Manage
Lead
Represent
Industry
Role
Project
Goal
Challenge

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Gleice lives in Santo Andre. She works at Sanofi Medley. She manages a team of thirty-four people. Her role is Commercial Excellence Manager. She leads the transformation department. Gleice has a goal: she wants to represent international brands. She does not give up. Every day, she challenges herself to improve.

1. Where does Gleice live?
A She lives in Sao Paulo city.
B She lives in Santo Andre.
C She lives in Campinas.
2. How many people does Gleice manage?
A She manages twenty people.
B She manages ten people.
C She manages thirty-four people.
3. What is Gleice's goal?
A She wants to work in the United States.
B She wants to represent international brands.
C She wants to manage a bigger team.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Present Simple = facts, habits, routines. Use it to talk about things that are always true or happen regularly.

FormStructureExample
AffirmativeI/You/We/They + verb"I manage a team."
Affirmative (3rd)He/She/It + verb+s"She manages a team."
NegativeI + do not + verb"I do not work alone."
QuestionDo/Does + subject + verb?"Does she lead the team?"

Common Mistake:

"She manage a team."

"She manages a team."

With he/she/it, always add -s or -es to the verb.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. If you get it wrong, read the hint carefully.

"I a team of thirty-four people."
"My is to represent international brands."
"She the Commercial Excellence team."
"The pharmaceutical is growing fast."
"My biggest is speaking in real time."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put these sentences in the correct order to make a self-introduction. Use the arrows or drag.

?"I manage a team of thirty-four people."
?"Good morning. My name is Gleice."
?"My goal is to represent international brands in Brazil."
?"I am from Santo Andre, Brazil."
?"I work in the pharmaceutical industry."

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Listen first, then record yourself. The system compares your words to the original.

My name is Gleice. I am from Santo Andre, Brazil.
I manage a team of thirty-four people at Sanofi Medley.
I work in the pharmaceutical industry.
My goal is to speak English with confidence.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best answer for each situation. Think about what sounds natural.

1. You meet a potential business partner. They ask: "What do you do?" What do you say?
A "I do business things."
B "I manage the Commercial Excellence team at Sanofi Medley."
C "My job is very difficult."
2. Someone asks: "Where are you from?" What is the best response?
A "I come from the Brazil."
B "Brazil is my country."
C "I am from Santo Andre, Brazil."
3. A colleague asks about your team. What sounds most professional?
A "I lead a team of thirty-four people in the pharmaceutical industry."
B "I have many persons in my group."
C "My team is big."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection
Imagine you are at an international pharmaceutical conference. Someone walks up to you and says: "Hi! Tell me about yourself." Record your response in English. There is no right answer. Just speak.

Survival Card -- Lesson 1

1My name is Gleice. I am from Santo Andre.
2I manage a team of thirty-four people.
3I work in the pharmaceutical industry.
4My goal is to represent international brands.
5Could you repeat that, please?
Aula 02 -- Pre-class

My World at Sanofi

Job & Team: Present simple 3rd person & Possessives
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Look at each word, its definition, and the example. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Department -- a division or section of a company
"My department is Commercial Excellence."
Responsibility -- a duty or task you must do
"Her responsibility is to lead transformation projects."
Report (to) -- to give information to someone above you
"I report to the Commercial Director."
Colleague -- a person you work with
"My colleagues and I improve commercial processes."
Process -- a series of steps to complete a task
"We are changing our sales processes."
Transformation -- a big change in how something works
"The transformation of our department is going well."
Strategy -- a plan to achieve a long-term goal
"Our strategy is to expand into new markets."
Performance -- how well someone or something works
"The team's performance has been strong this year."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word to its definition. Take your time.

Department
Responsibility
Report (to)
Colleague
Process
Transformation
Strategy
Performance

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Gleice works at Sanofi Medley. Her department is Commercial Excellence. She manages a team of thirty-four colleagues. Her main responsibility is to lead transformation projects. She reports to the Commercial Director. His role is to oversee their strategy. Our processes improve every quarter. The team's performance has been strong this year. Their transformation is going well.

1. Who does Gleice report to?
A She reports to the CEO.
B She reports to the Commercial Director.
C She reports to the HR Manager.
2. How many colleagues does Gleice have on her team?
A She has twenty colleagues.
B She has twelve colleagues.
C She has thirty-four colleagues.
3. What is Gleice's main responsibility?
A To lead transformation projects.
B To manage the budget.
C To hire new employees.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Possessives show who owns or is connected to something. Use the correct possessive before the noun.

SubjectPossessiveExample
Imy"My department is Commercial Excellence."
youyour"What are your main responsibilities?"
hehis"His role is to oversee the strategy."
sheher"Her responsibility is to lead projects."
weour"Our processes are improving."
theytheir"Their performance is strong."

3rd Person -s Rule: With he/she/it, add -s or -es to the verb. "She reports to the Director." "He manages the team."

Common Mistake:

"She department is Commercial Excellence."

"Her department is Commercial Excellence."

Use possessives (my, your, his, her, our, their) before nouns, not subject pronouns.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. If you get it wrong, read the hint carefully.

"My has thirty-four colleagues."
" responsibility is to oversee our strategy."
"I to the Commercial Director every week."
"The of our processes is almost complete."
"Our team's is strong this year."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put these sentences in the correct order to describe your team structure. Use the arrows or drag.

?"My responsibility is to lead transformation projects."
?"Our team's performance is strong this year."
?"My department is Commercial Excellence."
?"I report to the Commercial Director."
?"We have thirty-four colleagues on our team."

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Listen first, then record yourself. The system compares your words to the original.

My department is Commercial Excellence. We have thirty-four colleagues.
My responsibility is to lead transformation projects.
I report to the Commercial Director.
Our team's performance has been strong this year.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best answer for each situation. Think about what sounds natural.

1. A visitor asks: "Can you tell me about your department?" What do you say?
A "I work in a place with many people."
B "My department is Commercial Excellence. We have thirty-four colleagues."
C "The department is okay."
2. Someone asks: "Who do you report to?" What is the best response?
A "I report to nobody."
B "I report to the Commercial Director. His role is to oversee our strategy."
C "My boss is above me."
3. A colleague asks about your team's results. What sounds most professional?
A "Our performance is strong. We are meeting all our goals this year."
B "We are doing good things."
C "The team is nice."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection
Imagine a visiting international partner asks: "Tell me about your team at Sanofi." Describe your department, your colleagues, your responsibility, and who you report to. Record your response in English.

Survival Card -- Lesson 2

1My department is Commercial Excellence.
2My responsibility is to lead transformation projects.
3I report to the Commercial Director.
4Our team has thirty-four colleagues.
5Our performance is strong this year.
Aula 03 -- Pre-class

A Day in My Life

Routines: Present simple & Frequency adverbs
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Look at each word, its definition, and the example. Tap Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Schedule -- a plan that shows when things will happen
"My schedule is very busy this week."
Meeting -- a time when people come together to discuss something
"I have three meetings every day."
Deadline -- the last day or time to complete something
"The deadline for the report is Friday."
Review -- to look at something again carefully
"I review our sales numbers every morning."
Commute -- to travel between home and work
"I commute from Santo Andre to the office."
Routine -- things you do regularly, in the same order
"My morning routine starts at six."
Prioritize -- to decide which tasks are most important
"I always prioritize my team's needs."
Coordinate -- to organize people or activities to work together
"I coordinate projects across departments."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word to its definition. Take your time.

Schedule
Meeting
Deadline
Review
Commute
Routine
Prioritize
Coordinate

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Gleice usually starts her day at seven in the morning. She always reviews her schedule first. Then she checks her emails and prioritizes her tasks. She always has at least three meetings every day. She coordinates projects with different departments. She never misses a deadline. She commutes from Santo Andre. It usually takes about forty minutes. Her routine is very busy, but she loves it.

1. When does Gleice usually start her day?
A She starts at nine.
B She usually starts at seven.
C She starts at eight thirty.
2. Does Gleice ever miss a deadline?
A Yes, she sometimes misses deadlines.
B Yes, but only on Fridays.
C No, she never misses a deadline.
3. What does Gleice always do first in the morning?
A She reviews her schedule.
B She has a meeting.
C She commutes to work.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Frequency adverbs tell us how often something happens. They go between the subject and the main verb.

SubjectAdverbVerbExample
Ialways (100%)review"I always review my schedule."
Sheusually (80%)starts"She usually starts at seven."
Weoften (60%)have"We often have meetings."
Hesometimes (40%)works"He sometimes works late."
Theynever (0%)miss"They never miss a deadline."

Time expressions go at the end: "I have meetings every day." / "She starts in the morning." / "I review reports at night."

Common Mistake:

"I go always to meetings."

"I always go to meetings."

The adverb goes between the subject and the verb, not after the verb.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. If you get it wrong, read the hint carefully.

"I always my schedule in the morning."
"She usually has three every day."
"I never miss a at work."
"He sometimes by train."
"We always our projects together."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put these sentences in the correct order to describe your daily routine. Use the arrows or drag.

?"I prioritize my tasks for the day."
?"I coordinate projects with different departments."
?"I commute from Santo Andre to the office."
?"I have three meetings before lunch."
?"I review my schedule and check emails."

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Listen first, then record yourself. The system compares your words to the original.

I usually start my day at seven in the morning.
I always prioritize my team's needs.
I have three meetings every day.
I commute from Santo Andre to the office.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best answer for each situation. Think about what sounds natural.

1. A colleague asks: "How do you start your day?" What do you say?
A "I do morning things."
B "I usually start at seven. I review my schedule and prioritize my tasks."
C "My morning is busy."
2. Someone asks: "Do you have many meetings?" What is the best response?
A "Yes, too much meetings."
B "Yes, I always have at least three meetings a day."
C "Meetings are normal."
3. A partner asks about your commute. What sounds most natural?
A "I commute from Santo Andre. It usually takes about forty minutes."
B "I go from my house to work."
C "The travel is long."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection
Imagine a colleague from the London office asks: "What does your typical day look like?" Describe your daily routine using frequency adverbs (always, usually, sometimes, never). Talk about your commute, meetings, schedule, and deadlines. Record your response in English.

Survival Card -- Lesson 3

1I usually start my day at seven in the morning.
2I always prioritize my team's needs.
3I have three meetings every day.
4I never miss a deadline.
5I commute from Santo Andre to the office.
Aula 04 -- Pre-class

Questions That Connect

Ask and answer questions to meet people: question, answer, industry, role, project, repeat, spell, mean. Structures: "Wh + do/does + subject + verb?" and "Do you...?"
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example.

Question -- a sentence that asks for information
"Can I ask a question?" (Pergunta)
Answer -- what you say back; a reply
"That is a good answer." (Resposta)
Industry -- the type of business you work in
"I work in the pharma industry." (Setor / indústria)
Role -- your job or position in a team
"What is your role?" (Função / cargo)
Project -- a piece of work with a goal
"I work on a new project." (Projeto)
Repeat -- to say something again
"Could you repeat that?" (Repetir)
Spell -- to say the letters of a word
"How do you spell it?" (Soletrar)
Mean -- what a word or idea is about
"What does it mean?" (Significar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Question
Answer
Industry
Role
Project
Repeat
Spell
Mean

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Gleice is at a conference. She meets a new person and starts a conversation. What do you do? she asks. Where do you work? The man works in logistics. What does his company do? It moves products to stores. His name is difficult, so Gleice asks: How do you spell that? Good questions connect people. At the end, she asks: Do you have a business card?

1. Como Gleice pergunta a profissão? / How does Gleice ask about his job?
A "You do what work?"
B "What do you do?"
C "What you do?"
2. Como ela pergunta sobre a empresa (3a pessoa)? / How does she ask about the company?
A "What does his company do?"
B "What do his company does?"
C "What his company do?"
3. Como ela pede para soletrar? / How does she ask to spell?
A "How you spell that?"
B "How do you spell that?"
C "How does you spell that?"

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Wh-Questions

GRAMMAR

A fórmula da pergunta. / The question formula.

Structure / EstruturaUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
Wh + do + you + verb?Perguntar para "you/I/we/they" / Ask peopleWhere do you work?
Wh + does + he/she/it + verb?3a pessoa: usa DOES, verbo sem -s / 3rd personWhat does she do?
Do you + verb? (Yes/No)Pergunta de sim/não / Yes-No questionDo you have a card?

Lembre: depois de does, o verbo fica sem -s. / After does, no -s on the verb.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each question. Tap Listen to hear it.

" do you work?"
"What you do?"
"How do you your name?"
" do you like your job?"
"What she do?"
"Do you have any ?"

Stage 2: Put the Steps in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the conversation steps in order.

?He answers and asks her a question back.
?Gleice meets a new person at the conference.
?They exchange business cards.
?She asks: What do you do?
?She tells him where she works.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Pratique as perguntas que você vai usar para conhecer pessoas.

Where do you work?
What is your role?
How do you spell that?
What projects do you work on?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best question for each situation.

You want to know a new contact's job. / Você quer saber a profissão.
A "What do you do?"
B "What you are job?"
C "Do what you?"
You did not hear the name. / Você não ouviu o nome.
A "Repeat please name?"
B "How do you spell that?"
C "What is spell?"
You ask about her company (she). / Você pergunta sobre a empresa dela.
A "What do her company do?"
B "What does her company do?"
C "What her company does?"
You want to end with an open question. / Você quer terminar com uma pergunta aberta.
A "What do you like about your job?"
B "You like job?"
C "Job good you?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

No right or wrong. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Imagine you meet a new person at a Sanofi event. Ask FIVE questions to get to know them: their job, their company, their role, their projects, and how to spell their name. Use: What do you do?, Where do you work?, What does your company do?, How do you spell that?

Survival Card -- Lesson 4

1Can I ask you a question?Posso te fazer uma pergunta?
2What do you do?O que você faz (de trabalho)?
3Where do you work?Onde você trabalha?
4How do you spell that?Como se soletra isso?
5Could you repeat that, please?Poderia repetir, por favor?
Aula 5 -- Pre-class

Block 1 Review -- Confidence Check

Revisão das Aulas 1-4: quem você é, seu time na Sanofi, sua rotina e as perguntas que conectam. Estruturas: "I am... / I work at...", "She works... / My team has...", "I always... / I usually...", "What do you do?". Missão: a simulação integrada — a visita do Marc (Sanofi France).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Palavras de revisão das Aulas 1-4. Ouça cada uma e leia o exemplo.

Department -- a group inside a company
"Our department works with strategy." (Departamento)
Role -- the job you do inside the team
"What is your role at Sanofi?" (Função / cargo)
Project -- work with a goal, a team, and a deadline
"We work on a transformation project." (Projeto)
Performance -- how well a person or team works
"Our team's performance is excellent." (Desempenho)
Strategy -- the plan to win in the market
"I work with strategy and performance." (Estratégia)
Routine -- the things you do every day, in order
"My morning routine starts with coffee." (Rotina)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Combine cada palavra com a tradução.

Department
Role
Project
Performance
Strategy
Routine

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas.

Gleice has a big day today. Marc, a manager from Sanofi France, visits her office. Gleice is ready: "I am Gleice. I work at Sanofi Medley. I am the commercial excellence manager." She presents her team: "My team has five people. Ana works in logistics." She talks about her routine: "I always review my schedule in the morning." And she connects: "Marc, what do you do at Sanofi France?"

1. Como Gleice fala do trabalho da Ana? / How does Gleice talk about Ana's work?
A "Ana work in logistics."
B "Ana works in logistics."
C "Ana is work logistics."
2. O que Gleice SEMPRE faz de manhã? / What does Gleice always do in the morning?
A She reviews her schedule.
B She visits Sanofi France.
C She has lunch with Marc.
3. Como Gleice pergunta sobre o trabalho do Marc? / How does Gleice ask about Marc's job?
A "What you do?"
B "What do you do?"
C "What is you do?"

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- As 4 Ferramentas do Bloco 1

GRAMMAR

Revisão das estruturas das Aulas 1-4. / Review of the structures from Lessons 1-4.

Structure / EstruturaUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
I am... / I work at...Quem você é / Who you areI am Gleice. I work at Sanofi Medley.
she works / my team hasSeu time (3ª pessoa + possessivos) / Your teamShe works in logistics. My team has five people.
always / usually / neverSua rotina / Your routineI always review my schedule.
What/Where + do + you...?Perguntas que conectam / Connecting questionsWhat do you do? Where do you work?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase completa.

"I at Sanofi Medley."
"She in logistics."
"My team five people."
"I review my schedule in the morning."
"What you do?"
"What is your at Sanofi?"

Stage 2: Put the Visit in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque a visita do Marc na ordem certa.

?She talks about her routine and her projects.
?Marc arrives at the Santo André office.
?They plan the next steps together.
?Gleice welcomes him and introduces her team.
?Marc asks questions about the projects.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Pratique as frases da simulação.

Welcome to Sanofi Medley.
Bem-vindo à Sanofi Medley.
I am the commercial excellence manager.
Sou a gerente de excelência comercial.
My team has five people.
Meu time tem cinco pessoas.
What projects do you work on?
Em que projetos você trabalha?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

Um visitante da França chega ao seu escritório. / A visitor from France arrives at your office.
A "Welcome to Sanofi Medley! Nice to meet you."
B "You are here. Good."
C "Who are you?"
Ele pergunta sobre o seu time. / He asks about your team.
A "My team have five people."
B "My team has five people."
C "Five people is my team."
Ele pergunta sobre o seu dia. / He asks about your day.
A "I review always my schedule."
B "I always review my schedule in the morning."
C "Always I am review my schedule."
Você quer saber o que ELE faz. / You want to know what HE does.
A "What you do?"
B "What is you do?"
C "What do you do?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Não tem certo ou errado aqui. Pense em inglês, toque no microfone e fale livremente.

Pratique a simulação: Marc chega ao seu escritório. Dê as boas-vindas, diga quem você é e qual é a sua função, descreva seu time, fale da sua rotina e faça UMA pergunta para ele. Use: Welcome to..., I am..., I work at..., My team has..., I always..., What do you...?

Survival Card -- Lesson 5

1Welcome to Sanofi Medley!Bem-vindo à Sanofi Medley!
2I am the commercial excellence manager.Sou a gerente de excelência comercial.
3My team has five people.Meu time tem cinco pessoas.
4I always review my schedule in the morning.Eu sempre reviso minha agenda de manhã.
5What projects do you work on?Em que projetos você trabalha?
Aula 06 -- Pre-class

My Career Story -- Simple Past (Regular Verbs)

Tell the story of where you started. New vocabulary: start, study, join, graduate, move, apply, career, promotion. The simple past of regular verbs: add -ed (started, joined), +d after e (moved), and y to ied (studied, applied).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Start -- to begin a job, a career, or a task
"I started my career in a small office." (começar)
Study -- to learn about a subject, often at school or at night
"I studied at night for two years." (estudar)
Join -- to become part of a company or a team
"I joined Sanofi in 2015." (entrar para)
Graduate -- to finish a university course and get a degree
"I graduated from university in 2010." (formar-se)
Move -- to change to a new place, city, or team
"I moved to a new team last year." (mudar de)
Apply -- to formally ask for a job or a position
"I applied for a new position." (candidatar-se)
Career -- your professional life and journey over the years
"My career grew step by step." (carreira)
Promotion -- a move to a higher or better position at work
"I earned a promotion last year." (promoção)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Start
Study
Join
Graduate
Move
Apply
Career
Promotion

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Marcos started his career in 2009 in a small office. He studied at night and worked during the day. In 2013 he joined a pharmaceutical company as a junior analyst. Two years later, he moved to another city for a better position. He worked hard and learned a lot. Last year he applied for a manager role and earned a promotion.

1. When did Marcos start his career?
A He started in 2009.
B He started in 2013.
C He never started.
2. What did Marcos do at night?
A He traveled.
B He studied.
C He slept all day.
3. What happened last year?
A He left the company.
B He started university.
C He applied for a manager role and earned a promotion.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Simple Past (Regular Verbs)

GRAMMAR

Para falar do passado, a maioria dos verbos ganha a terminação -ed, e a forma é a mesma para todos os sujeitos. / To talk about the past, most verbs add -ed, and the form is the same for every subject.

Rule / RegraBasePast / Passado
most verbs: + edstart, joinstarted, joined
ends in e: + dmove, graduatemoved, graduated
consonant + y: y to iedstudy, applystudied, applied
Regra de ouroa forma do passado é igual para I, you, he, she, we, they: "She started", "We started".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct past verb. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"I my career in a small office."
"She at night for two years."
"In 2015 I Sanofi."
"I from university in 2010."
"We to a new team."
"Last year I for a promotion."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the career events in the correct order.

?She joined a bigger company.
?She finished school and started her first job.
?She applied for a promotion and earned it.
?She studied at night to learn more.
?She moved to a new city for the role.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I started my career in 2010.
Comecei minha carreira em 2010.
I studied English at night.
Estudei inglês à noite.
I joined Sanofi a few years ago.
Entrei para a Sanofi alguns anos atrás.
Last year I applied for a promotion.
Ano passado me candidatei a uma promoção.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each career moment.

You want to say that you began your first job in 2011. What do you say?
A "I start my first job in 2011."
B "I started my first job in 2011."
C "I starting my first job in 2011."
You want to say that you learned at night. What do you say?
A "I studied at night."
B "I studyed at night."
C "I study at night yesterday."
You want to say that you got a better position last year. What do you say?
A "Last year I apply for a promotion."
B "Last year I applyed for a promotion."
C "Last year I applied for a promotion."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Tell your career story in five sentences. Say when you started, what you studied, when you joined your company, where you moved, and the last position you applied for. Use the simple past with -ed.

Survival Card -- Lesson 6

1I started my career in 2010.Comecei minha carreira em 2010.
2I studied a lot to grow.Estudei muito para crescer.
3I joined Sanofi a few years ago.Entrei para a Sanofi alguns anos atrás.
4I moved to a new team.Mudei para uma equipe nova.
5Last year I applied for a promotion.Ano passado me candidatei a uma promoção.
Aula 07 -- Pre-class

Key Moments -- Simple Past (Irregular Verbs)

Tell the turning points of your career. New vocabulary: go, get, make, leave, become, take, win, find. The simple past of irregular verbs does not add -ed: go to went, get to got, make to made. After did, use the base verb (Did you go?).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each verb and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Go -- to travel to a place or an event (past: went)
"I went to a conference in 2018." (ir)
Get -- to receive or obtain something (past: got)
"I got my first job in 2009." (conseguir / obter)
Make -- to create something or take a decision (past: made)
"I made an important decision." (fazer / tomar)
Leave -- to go away from a place or a job (past: left)
"I left my old company in 2015." (sair / deixar)
Become -- to start to be something new (past: became)
"I became a manager last year." (tornar-se)
Take -- to accept or choose a role or a chance (past: took)
"I took a new role at Sanofi." (assumir / aceitar)
Win -- to be the best and receive a prize (past: won)
"My team won an award." (ganhar / vencer)
Find -- to discover something for the first time (past: found)
"I found a great opportunity." (encontrar / achar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each verb with its translation.

Go
Get
Make
Leave
Become
Take
Win
Find

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Paulo got his first job in 2009 at a small company, and he found a real passion for the work. In 2013 he made a big decision and left for a bigger company. He went to another city and became a team leader. Two years ago, his team won an important award.

1. When did Paulo get his first job?
A He got it in 2009.
B He got it in 2013.
C He never got a job.
2. What did Paulo do in 2013?
A He won an award.
B He made a decision and left for a bigger company.
C He stopped working.
3. What happened two years ago?
A He started university.
B He left the country.
C His team won an important award.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Simple Past (Irregular Verbs)

GRAMMAR

Alguns verbos não ganham -ed no passado: eles mudam de forma e você memoriza cada um. Para negativa e pergunta, use did + o verbo na base. / Some verbs do not add -ed in the past: they change shape and you memorize each one. For negatives and questions, use did + the base verb.

BasePast / PassadoBasePast / Passado
gowentbecomebecame
getgottaketook
makemadewinwon
leaveleftfindfound
Regra de ouroa forma do passado é igual para I, you, he, she, we, they. Depois de did, use a base: "Did you go?" (não "Did you went?").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct irregular past verb. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"In 2009 I my first job."
"She a big decision in 2014."
"We to a conference last year."
"They the company in 2018."
"I a manager at Sanofi."
"My team an award last month."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the key moments in the correct order.

?She went to another city and became a manager.
?She got her first job at a small company.
?She found a new passion and took a leadership role.
?She made a big decision and left for a new role.
?Her team won an important award that year.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I got my first job in 2009.
Consegui meu primeiro emprego em 2009.
I made a big decision.
Tomei uma grande decisão.
I went to a new city for the role.
Fui para uma cidade nova pelo cargo.
Last year my team won an award.
Ano passado meu time ganhou um prêmio.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each career moment.

You want to say that you received your first job in 2011. What do you say?
A "I get my first job in 2011."
B "I got my first job in 2011."
C "I getted my first job in 2011."
You want to say that you traveled to a conference. What do you say?
A "I went to a conference."
B "I goed to a conference."
C "I go to a conference yesterday."
You want to ask a colleague if she attended the meeting. What do you say?
A "Did you went to the meeting?"
B "Do you went to the meeting?"
C "Did you go to the meeting?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Tell three key moments of your career in five sentences. Say when you got your first job, the decision you made, where you went, the role you took, and the win you found. Use the simple past with irregular verbs.

Survival Card -- Lesson 7

1I got my first job in 2010.Consegui meu primeiro emprego em 2010.
2I made a big decision and left.Tomei uma grande decisão e saí.
3I went to a new city.Fui para uma cidade nova.
4I became a manager at Sanofi.Tornei-me gerente na Sanofi.
5Last year my team won an award.Ano passado meu time ganhou um prêmio.
Aula 08 -- Pre-class

The Pharma World -- Industry Vocabulary (Present Simple)

Talk about your world at Sanofi. New vocabulary: patient, treatment, product, market, pharmacy, brand, supply, launch. With he, she, it and singular nouns (the company, a patient), the present simple verb adds -s: Sanofi develops, the team supplies. After does, use the base verb (Does the company sell?).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Patient -- a person who receives medical care
"A patient needs good treatment." (paciente)
Treatment -- medical care that helps a patient get better
"This treatment helps many patients." (tratamento)
Product -- a thing a company makes and sells
"Sanofi makes a safe product." (produto)
Market -- the people who buy and sell products
"The market wants new medicines." (mercado)
Pharmacy -- a store that sells medicines
"The pharmacy sells our medicine." (farmácia)
Brand -- the name and image of a product
"Our brand is strong and trusted." (marca)
Supply -- to give products to stores and customers
"We supply many pharmacies." (fornecer / abastecer)
Launch -- to put a new product on the market
"The company launches a new product." (lançar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Patient
Treatment
Product
Market
Pharmacy
Brand
Supply
Launch

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Renata works for a big pharma brand. Her company develops new medicines and supplies many pharmacies. Every day, the team studies the market and helps doctors. When the company launches a new product, every pharmacy sells it to patients. A patient always needs good treatment, so the brand works hard.

1. Where does Renata work?
A She works for a big pharma brand.
B She works in a pharmacy.
C She works in a hospital.
2. What happens when the company launches a new product?
A The team studies a book.
B Every pharmacy sells it to patients.
C The brand closes.
3. What does a patient always need?
A A new market.
B A new brand.
C Good treatment.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Simple (he / she / it + -s)

GRAMMAR

Com he, she, it e nomes no singular (the company, a patient), o verbo no presente ganha -s: Sanofi develops, the team supplies. Verbos terminados em consoante + y trocam para -ies: supply vira supplies. Para negativa e pergunta, use does + o verbo na base. / With he, she, it and singular nouns, the present simple verb adds -s. Verbs ending in consonant + y change to -ies. For negatives and questions, use does + the base verb.

BaseHe / She / ItBaseHe / She / It
developdevelopsneedneeds
sellsellsmakemakes
supplysupplieslaunchlaunches
Regra de ouroafirmativa: Sanofi develops. Negativa e pergunta: Sanofi does not develop / Does Sanofi develop? (depois de does, use a base).

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct present simple verb. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Sanofi new medicines."
"The pharmacy our brand."
"Our team many pharmacies."
"A patient good treatment."
"The company a new product."
"Does the market new medicines?"

Stage 2: Put the Product Journey in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?It launches the product on the market.
?The company develops a new product in the laboratory.
?The patient gets the treatment and feels better.
?It tests the treatment for many months.
?The team supplies the medicine to pharmacies.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

My company develops new medicines.
Minha empresa desenvolve novos medicamentos.
We supply many pharmacies.
Nós abastecemos muitas farmácias.
The pharmacy sells our product.
A farmácia vende nosso produto.
We launch a new brand this year.
Nós lançamos uma nova marca este ano.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation at work.

You want to say what Sanofi does. What do you say?
A "Sanofi develop new medicines."
B "Sanofi develops new medicines."
C "Sanofi developing new medicines."
You want to ask what a pharmacy sells. What do you say?
A "Does the pharmacy sells our product?"
B "Do the pharmacy sell our product?"
C "Does the pharmacy sell our product?"
You want to say the team gives products to stores. What do you say?
A "The team supplies many pharmacies."
B "The team supplys many pharmacies."
C "The team supply many pharmacies."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe your world at Sanofi in five sentences. Say what the company develops, what your team supplies, who needs the product, what the brand does, and when the company launches a new treatment. Use the present simple with -s.

Survival Card -- Lesson 8

1My company develops new medicines.Minha empresa desenvolve novos medicamentos.
2We supply many pharmacies every week.Nós abastecemos muitas farmácias toda semana.
3A patient needs good treatment.Um paciente precisa de bom tratamento.
4Our brand is strong and trusted.Nossa marca é forte e confiável.
5The company launches a new product this year.A empresa lança um novo produto este ano.
Aula 09 -- Pre-class

Meetings at Sanofi -- Basic Meeting Language (Can / Could)

Ask and offer politely in your meetings. New vocabulary: agenda, attend, suggest, agree, decision, topic, postpone, update. Use can and could to ask, offer, and suggest: Can you send the agenda? Could you repeat that? After can and could, the verb is always in the base form -- no -s and no to: she can attend (not attends, not to attend).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Agenda -- the list of topics for a meeting
"Can you send the agenda?" (pauta da reunião)
Attend -- to go to a meeting
"I attend the meeting every Monday." (comparecer / participar)
Suggest -- to give an idea to the group
"I suggest a short break." (sugerir)
Agree -- to have the same opinion
"We agree with the plan." (concordar)
Decision -- a choice the team makes together
"The team makes a good decision." (decisão)
Topic -- a subject you talk about in a meeting
"The first topic is the new product." (tópico / assunto)
Postpone -- to move a meeting to a later time
"Can we postpone the meeting?" (adiar)
Update -- new information about progress
"I have an update for the team." (atualização)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Agenda
Attend
Suggest
Agree
Decision
Topic
Postpone
Update

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Renata runs a good meeting. Before they start, she sends the agenda, so everyone knows the topics. In the meeting, she says: "Can you give us a short update?" and "Could you repeat that, please?" When a topic is difficult, the team says: "We could postpone it." Everyone can suggest one idea. At the end, they agree and make a decision.

1. What does Renata send before the meeting?
A She sends the agenda with the topics.
B She sends a new product.
C She sends a pharmacy.
2. How does she ask politely to repeat?
A "Could you repeats that?"
B "Could you repeat that, please?"
C "Could you to repeat that?"
3. What does the team do at the end?
A They postpone every topic.
B They close the pharmacy.
C They agree and make a decision.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Can / Could (+ base verb)

GRAMMAR

Use can e could para pedir, oferecer e sugerir em reuniões. Depois de can e could, o verbo fica sempre na BASE: sem -s e sem to. Diga "Can you send the agenda?" e "She can attend", nunca "can to send" ou "can attends". Could é mais educado e suave que can. Negativa: cannot e could not. / Use can and could to ask, offer, and suggest in meetings. After can and could, the verb is always in the base form: no -s and no to. Could is softer and more polite than can.

UseExample (base verb)
Ask (can)Can you send the agenda?
Ask politely (could)Could you repeat that, please?
Offer / abilityI can attend the meeting.
Suggest softlyWe could postpone the meeting.
Regra de ourodepois de can e could, use a base (sem -s, sem to): she can attend, não "can attends" nem "can to attend".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the base verb after can or could. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Can you the agenda?"
"Could you the last topic?"
"She can the meeting."
"We could the meeting."
"Can everyone one idea?"
"I cannot next week."

Stage 2: Put the Meeting in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?The team discusses each topic.
?The manager sends the agenda to the team.
?The manager writes the update after the meeting.
?Everyone attends the meeting on time.
?Everyone agrees and makes a decision.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Can you send the agenda, please?
Você pode enviar a pauta, por favor?
Could you repeat that, please?
Você poderia repetir isso, por favor?
I have a short update for the team.
Eu tenho uma breve atualização para a equipe.
We could postpone the meeting to next week.
Nós poderíamos adiar a reunião para a próxima semana.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation in a meeting.

You want the agenda from David. What do you say?
A "Can you to send the agenda?"
B "Can you send the agenda, please?"
C "Can you sends the agenda?"
You want to ask politely to postpone. What do you say?
A "Could we postpone the meeting?"
B "Could we postpones the meeting?"
C "Could we to postpone the meeting?"
You say you are free to go to Monday's meeting. What do you say?
A "I can attends the meeting on Monday."
B "I can to attend the meeting on Monday."
C "I can attend the meeting on Monday."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Open a short meeting at Sanofi in five sentences. Ask for the agenda, ask for an update with could, suggest one idea, ask if everyone can attend, and make a decision. Use can and could with the base verb.

Survival Card -- Lesson 9

1Can you send the agenda, please?Você pode enviar a pauta, por favor?
2Could you repeat that, please?Você poderia repetir isso, por favor?
3I have a short update for the team.Eu tenho uma breve atualização para a equipe.
4We could postpone the meeting to next week.Nós poderíamos adiar a reunião para a próxima semana.
5I agree. That is our final decision.Eu concordo. Essa é a nossa decisão final.
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Block 2 Review -- Progress Check (aulas 6 to 9)

A progress check of block 2, all together: your career story in the simple past (started, studied, got, became), your pharma world in the present simple (the company supplies pharmacies), and your meetings with can and could (could you send the agenda?). Review words: career, promotion, become, win, product, market, agenda, decision. No new grammar -- we make what you learned strong.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Review the eight key words from block 2. Tap Listen to hear each one.

Career -- your professional life and jobs over time
"I started my career ten years ago." (carreira)
Promotion -- a move to a higher or better job
"I got a promotion last year." (promoção)
Become -- to start to be something new (past: became)
"I became a team manager." (tornar-se)
Win -- to get first place or a new contract (past: won)
"We won two new contracts." (vencer)
Product -- something a company makes and sells
"We launched a new product." (produto)
Market -- the people who buy and sell products
"The market answered well." (mercado)
Agenda -- the list of topics for a meeting
"Can you send the agenda?" (pauta)
Decision -- a choice the team makes together
"We make a final decision." (decisão)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Career
Promotion
Become
Win
Product
Market
Agenda
Decision

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Renata started her career as a sales assistant. She studied at night and worked during the day. After three years, she got a promotion and became a manager. Today, her team supplies medicines to many pharmacies. Every week, she sends the agenda and runs a meeting. She always asks: "Could you share an update?" At the end, the team agrees and makes a decision.

1. How did Renata start her career?
A She started as a sales assistant.
B She started as a manager.
C She started as a pharmacy.
2. What does her team do today?
A The team supply medicines.
B The team supplies medicines to pharmacies.
C The team studied at night.
3. How does she ask for an update politely?
A "Could you to share an update?"
B "Could you shares an update?"
C "Could you share an update?"

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Block 2 Toolbox

GRAMMAR

Revisão do bloco 2. Passado: verbos regulares ganham -ed (started, studied, worked); irregulares mudam (get-got, become-became, win-won). Em negativa e pergunta, use did + verbo na base (Did you start? não "Did you started?"). Presente simples: he/she/it/the company ganham -s (the company supplies). Can e could: sempre verbo na base (could you send?). / Block 2 review. Past: regular verbs add -ed; irregular verbs change. In negatives and questions, use did + base verb. Present simple adds -s for he/she/it. After can and could, use the base verb.

ToolExample
Past -- regular (+ -ed)I started, I studied, I worked.
Past -- irregular (changes)I got, I became, we won.
Past -- question (did + base)Did you start? Did the team win?
Present simple (+ -s)The company supplies pharmacies.
Can / could (+ base)Could you send the agenda?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct verb form. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Last year, I a new project."
"After three years, I a promotion."
"I a team manager."
"The company medicines to pharmacies."
"Could you the agenda?"
"Did the team the contract?"

Stage 2: Put the Journey in Order

Order

Listen first, then put Renata's journey in the correct order.

?She got a promotion and became a manager.
?Renata started her career as a sales assistant.
?Every week she runs a meeting and the team makes a decision.
?She studied at night and worked during the day.
?Her team launched a new product on the market.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I started my career ten years ago.
Eu comecei minha carreira há dez anos.
I got a promotion and became a manager.
Eu fui promovida e me tornei gerente.
The company supplies medicines to pharmacies.
A empresa fornece medicamentos às farmácias.
Could you send the agenda, please?
Você poderia enviar a pauta, por favor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You talk about a past key moment in your career. What do you say?
A "I becomed a manager."
B "I became a manager three years ago."
C "I become a manager three years ago."
You describe what your company does today. What do you say?
A "The company supplies medicines to pharmacies."
B "The company supply medicines to pharmacies."
C "The company supplied medicines yesterday only."
You ask David politely to send the agenda. What do you say?
A "Could you to send the agenda?"
B "Could you sends the agenda?"
C "Could you send the agenda, please?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Tell your block 2 story in five sentences. Start with your career in the past (started, studied, got a promotion, became a manager), then describe your team today in the present (the company supplies pharmacies), and finish with a polite request using could.

Survival Card -- Lesson 10

1I started my career ten years ago.Eu comecei minha carreira há dez anos.
2I got a promotion and became a manager.Eu fui promovida e me tornei gerente.
3We launched a new product on the market.Nós lançamos um novo produto no mercado.
4Could you send the agenda, please?Você poderia enviar a pauta, por favor?
5We agree and make a final decision.Nós concordamos e tomamos uma decisão final.
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

First Contact -- Phone & Email (Present Continuous)

Make first contact by phone and email. New vocabulary: call, ring, reply, reach, forward, attach, available, message. Use the present continuous to say what is happening right now: am / is / are + verb-ing. Say "I am calling", "she is answering the phone", "we are replying to the email". Never forget the verb to be (am/is/are) and the -ing.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Call -- to phone someone
"I am calling about the project." (ligar)
Ring -- when a phone makes a sound
"The phone is ringing." (tocar)
Reply -- to write back to an email or message
"I am replying to your email." (responder)
Reach -- to contact someone successfully
"I am trying to reach David." (contatar / alcançar)
Forward -- to send an email to another person
"I am forwarding the email to my team." (encaminhar)
Attach -- to add a file to an email
"I am attaching the report." (anexar)
Available -- free to talk or help right now
"Is David available now?" (disponível)
Message -- a short note you leave for someone
"Can I leave a message?" (mensagem / recado)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Call
Ring
Reply
Reach
Forward
Attach
Available
Message

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Renata is at her desk right now, and her phone is ringing. She is answering it, and she says her name first. At the moment, she is working with a new client, so she is emailing every day. Now she is replying to a message and is attaching a report to the email. When the client is not available, she leaves a message or she forwards the email to a colleague.

1. What is Renata doing right now?
A She is answering the phone and working with a client.
B She is leaving the office.
C She is starting a new career.
2. How is she contacting the new client?
A She emails once a month.
B She is emailing every day.
C She is not contacting the client.
3. What does she do when the client is not available?
A She closes the email.
B She stops working.
C She leaves a message or forwards the email.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Continuous (am / is / are + -ing)

GRAMMAR

Use o present continuous para ações acontecendo AGORA: am / is / are + verbo com -ing. Diga "I am calling", "she is answering", "we are replying". Nunca esqueça o verbo to be (am/is/are) nem o -ing: não diga "I calling" nem "she answer". Negativa: I am not calling, she is not working. Pergunta: Are you calling? Is she answering? / Use the present continuous for an action happening now: am / is / are + verb-ing. Never drop the verb to be or the -ing.

SubjectExample (am / is / are + -ing)
II am calling the client.
He / She / ItShe is answering the phone.
We / You / TheyWe are replying to the email.
Negative / QuestionHe is not working. / Are you calling?
Regra de ourosempre am / is / are + verbo com -ing: I am calling, não "I calling"; she is answering, não "she answer".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the verb in the -ing form. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Right now, I am the client."
"She is the phone."
"We are to your email."
"He is the report to the email."
"They are on the project now."
"I am the email to my team."

Stage 2: Put the Call in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?She takes a short message for her manager.
?The phone is ringing, and Gleice answers it.
?She attaches the report and forwards it to the team.
?She says her name and asks who is calling.
?She opens her email and replies to the client.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Hello, this is Gleice speaking.
Olá, aqui é a Gleice.
I am calling about the project.
Estou ligando sobre o projeto.
Can I leave a message, please?
Posso deixar um recado, por favor?
I am attaching the report to this email.
Estou anexando o relatório a este e-mail.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each first contact situation.

You phone David and he answers. How do you say what you are doing?
A "I calling about the project."
B "I am calling about the project."
C "I am call about the project."
David is at his desk and the phone rings. What is true now?
A "He answer the phone."
B "He are answering the phone."
C "He is answering the phone."
You want to ask David to send the email to the team. What do you say?
A "Could you forward it to the team, please?"
B "Could you to forward it to the team?"
C "Could you forwards it to the team?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Make a first contact call in five sentences. Answer the phone with your name, say you are calling about the project, say what your team is doing right now, ask if David is available, and ask him to forward the email. Use the present continuous: am / is / are + verb-ing.

Survival Card -- Lesson 11

1Hello, this is Gleice speaking.Olá, aqui é a Gleice.
2I am calling about the project.Estou ligando sobre o projeto.
3Can I leave a message, please?Posso deixar um recado, por favor?
4I am attaching the report to this email.Estou anexando o relatório a este e-mail.
5Could you forward it to the team, please?Você poderia encaminhar para a equipe, por favor?
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

My Business Idea -- Elevator Pitch (Going to)

Build a business idea and give a short elevator pitch. New vocabulary: idea, pitch, customer, problem, solve, service, grow, need. Use going to to talk about your future plans: am / is / are going to + verb. Say "I am going to launch my idea", "she is going to present her pitch", "my service is going to grow". Never forget the verb to be (am/is/are) or the word to.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Idea -- a new thought or plan for a business
"My idea is going to help busy people." (ideia)
Pitch -- a short talk to present and sell an idea
"My pitch is going to be short." (apresentação rápida)
Customer -- a person who buys or uses your service
"My customer is a pharmacy manager." (cliente)
Problem -- something difficult that you want to fix
"I am going to solve this problem." (problema)
Solve -- to find an answer to a problem
"My service is going to solve it." (resolver)
Service -- work or help you do for a customer
"I am going to launch a new service." (serviço)
Grow -- to get bigger or more successful
"My idea is going to grow." (crescer)
Need -- to want something because it is important
"My customers need fast reports." (precisar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Idea
Pitch
Customer
Problem
Solve
Service
Grow
Need

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Renata has a business idea. Next month, she is going to launch a small service for busy pharmacies. Her customers need fast reports, but they do not have time. Her service is going to solve this problem, because it is going to save them time. First, she is going to present her pitch to her team. Then she is going to explain the problem and show her idea. She is sure her idea is going to grow.

1. What is Renata going to launch next month?
A A small service for busy pharmacies.
B A new pharmacy in another city.
C A meeting with her manager.
2. What problem do her customers have?
A They have too many reports.
B They need fast reports, but they do not have time.
C They do not have customers.
3. Why is her service going to help?
A Because it is free.
B Because it is in many countries.
C Because it is going to save them time.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Going to (am / is / are going to + verb)

GRAMMAR

Use o going to para planos do futuro: am / is / are + going to + verbo na forma base. Diga "I am going to launch", "she is going to present", "we are going to solve". Nunca esqueça o verbo to be (am/is/are) nem o to, e use o verbo na forma base: não diga "I going to launch" nem "going to launching". Negativa: I am not going to wait. Pergunta: Are you going to present? / Use going to for a future plan: am / is / are + going to + base verb. Never drop the verb to be or the word to.

SubjectExample (am / is / are going to + verb)
II am going to launch my idea.
He / She / ItShe is going to present the pitch.
We / You / TheyWe are going to solve the problem.
Negative / QuestionHe is not going to wait. / Are you going to present?
Regra de ourosempre am / is / are + going to + verbo na forma base: I am going to launch, não "I going to launch" nem "going to launching".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the base verb after "going to". Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Next month, I am going to my idea."
"She is going to her pitch."
"My service is going to the problem."
"We are going to many customers."
"I think my idea is going to fast."
"They are going to my business idea."

Stage 2: Put the Pitch in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?She presents her business idea.
?Gleice says hello and her name.
?She asks the investor for support.
?She explains the problem her customer has.
?She says how her service is going to help.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Hello, I am going to present my business idea.
Olá, eu vou apresentar a minha ideia de negócio.
My customers need a faster service.
Meus clientes precisam de um serviço mais rápido.
My idea is going to solve this problem.
Minha ideia vai resolver este problema.
Are you going to support my idea?
Você vai apoiar a minha ideia?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each pitch situation.

You open your pitch and talk about your plan. What do you say?
A "I going to present my business idea."
B "I am going to present my business idea."
C "I am going to presenting my business idea."
David asks about your service. How do you say what it is going to do?
A "It going to solve the problem."
B "It is going to solving the problem."
C "It is going to solve the problem."
You want to ask David about his plan for next week. What do you say?
A "Are you going to present next week?"
B "You going to present next week?"
C "Are you going to presenting next week?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Give a short elevator pitch in five sentences. Say your name, present your business idea, say the problem your customer has, say how your service is going to solve it, and ask for support. Use going to: am / is / are going to + verb.

Survival Card -- Lesson 12

1Hello, I am going to present my business idea.Olá, eu vou apresentar a minha ideia de negócio.
2My customers need a faster service.Meus clientes precisam de um serviço mais rápido.
3My idea is going to solve this problem.Minha ideia vai resolver este problema.
4Next month, I am going to launch it.No próximo mês, eu vou lançá-lo.
5Are you going to support my idea?Você vai apoiar a minha ideia?
Aula 13 -- Pre-class

Describing Products -- What We Offer (Comparatives)

Describe a product and compare it with another one. New vocabulary: feature, quality, benefit, compare, effective, reliable, affordable, powerful. Use comparatives to compare two things: short adjective + er + than (faster than, cheaper than), more + adjective + than for long words (more reliable than, more effective than), and irregular forms (good becomes better). Never say "more faster" or "gooder".
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Feature -- a special part or function of a product
"Our product has one great feature." (recurso)
Quality -- how good a product is
"The quality of our product is high." (qualidade)
Benefit -- a good thing a product gives the customer
"The main benefit is that it saves time." (benefício)
Compare -- to look at two things to see how they are different
"Let me compare the two products." (comparar)
Effective -- it works well and gives good results
"This product is very effective." (eficaz)
Reliable -- you can trust it because it always works
"The new model is more reliable." (confiável)
Affordable -- not expensive; easy for a customer to pay for
"Our price is more affordable than before." (acessível)
Powerful -- very strong; it does a lot of work
"The new engine is more powerful." (potente)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Feature
Quality
Benefit
Compare
Effective
Reliable
Affordable
Powerful

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Renata sells a new product. She wants to compare it with the old model. The new model is faster than the old one, and it has a more powerful engine. The quality is high, and it is more reliable than before. The price is cheaper than last year, so it is more affordable for her customers. The main benefit is simple: it is more effective and it saves time. Renata thinks her new product is better than the others on the market.

1. How is the new model different in speed?
A It is faster than the old one.
B It is slower than the old one.
C It is the same speed.
2. Why is the new model more affordable?
A Because it is free.
B Because the price is cheaper than last year.
C Because it is more expensive.
3. What is the main benefit of the new product?
A It is bigger and heavier.
B It has more colors.
C It is more effective and it saves time.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Comparatives (adjective + er / more + adjective + than)

GRAMMAR

Para comparar duas coisas, use o comparativo + than. Adjetivo CURTO (1 sílaba): adjetivo + er (fast → faster, cheap → cheaper). Adjetivo terminado em y: troca por ier (easy → easier). Adjetivo LONGO (2+ sílabas): more + adjetivo (reliable → more reliable, effective → more effective, affordable → more affordable). Irregulares: good → better, bad → worse. Nunca use os dois juntos ("more faster") nem diga "gooder". / To compare two things, use the comparative + than. Short adjective: add -er. Long adjective: use more. Irregular: better, worse.

Type of adjectiveComparative + than
Short (fast, cheap)It is faster than / cheaper than the old one.
Ends in -y (easy)It is easier than before.
Long (reliable, effective)It is more reliable than / more effective than the others.
Irregular (good, bad)It is better than / worse than before.
Regra de ourocurto + er, longo + more, nunca os dois: diga faster (não "more faster") e better (não "gooder").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct comparative. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Our product is than the old one."
"This device is than the others."
"The new model is than the first one."
"Our service is than before."
"The price is than before."
"The new engine is than the old one."

Stage 2: Put the Description in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?She compares it with the old product.
?Gleice names the product and says what it is.
?She says why it is a better choice.
?She describes one strong feature.
?She explains the main benefit for the customer.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Our product has a great feature.
Nosso produto tem um ótimo recurso.
It is faster than the old one.
É mais rápido que o antigo.
It is more reliable and more effective.
É mais confiável e mais eficaz.
I think it is better than the others.
Eu acho que é melhor que os outros.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You compare the speed of your product with the old one. What do you say?
A "Our product is more faster than the old one."
B "Our product is faster than the old one."
C "Our product is fast than the old one."
You compare how much customers can trust it. How do you say it?
A "It is reliabler than the others."
B "It is more reliabler than the others."
C "It is more reliable than the others."
You give your opinion about your service. What do you say?
A "I think it is better than the others."
B "I think it is gooder than the others."
C "I think it is more better than the others."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe and compare a product in five sentences. Name the product, describe one feature, give two comparisons with the old one, and say the main benefit. Use comparatives: faster than, more reliable than, better than.

Survival Card -- Lesson 13

1Our product has a great feature.Nosso produto tem um ótimo recurso.
2It is faster than the old one.É mais rápido que o antigo.
3It is more reliable and more effective.É mais confiável e mais eficaz.
4The price is more affordable.O preço é mais acessível.
5I think it is better than the others.Eu acho que é melhor que os outros.
Aula 14 -- Pre-class

The Best Choice -- Why We Are the Best (Superlatives)

Recommend a product and say it is the best of all. New vocabulary: recommend, choice, leader, advantage, popular, valuable, option, winner. Use superlatives to say number one: the + short adjective + est (the fastest, the cheapest), the most + adjective for long words (the most reliable, the most popular), and irregular forms (good becomes the best). Never say "the most fast" or "the goodest".
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Recommend -- to tell someone that something is good and they should choose it
"I recommend this product to you." (recomendar)
Choice -- the thing a customer decides to pick
"It is the best choice for you." (escolha)
Leader -- the number one company in the market
"Our company is the market leader." (líder)
Advantage -- a good point that makes one product better than others
"Our main advantage is the quality." (vantagem)
Popular -- many people like it and buy it
"It is the most popular product." (popular)
Valuable -- worth a lot; very useful for the customer
"It is the most valuable solution." (valioso)
Option -- one of the things a customer can choose
"It is the best option on the market." (opção)
Winner -- the best one of all the products
"This product is the clear winner." (vencedor)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Recommend
Choice
Leader
Advantage
Popular
Valuable
Option
Winner

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Renata wants to recommend her product to a big customer. It is the fastest device on the market, and that is her main advantage. It is also the most reliable one, so it is the most popular brand for pharmacies. The price is good, so it is the most valuable solution and the cheapest good option. Her company is the market leader. She says her product is the best choice and the clear winner.

1. How fast is the product?
A It is the fastest device on the market.
B It is the slowest device on the market.
C It is slower than the old one.
2. Why is it the most popular brand?
A Because it is the most expensive.
B Because it is the most reliable one.
C Because it is the heaviest.
3. What does Renata say about her product in the end?
A It is one option among many.
B It is worse than the others.
C It is the best choice and the clear winner.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Superlatives (the + adjective + est / the most + adjective)

GRAMMAR

Para dizer que algo é número um (o melhor de todos), use the + superlativo. Adjetivo CURTO (1 sílaba): the + adjetivo + est (fast → the fastest, cheap → the cheapest). Adjetivo terminado em y: troca por iest (easy → the easiest). Adjetivo LONGO (2+ sílabas): the most + adjetivo (reliable → the most reliable, popular → the most popular, valuable → the most valuable). Irregulares: good → the best, bad → the worst. Nunca esqueça o "the", nunca use the most com adjetivo curto ("the most fast") nem diga "the goodest". / To say number one, use the + superlative. Short adjective: add -est. Long adjective: use the most. Irregular: the best, the worst.

Type of adjectiveThe + superlative
Short (fast, cheap)It is the fastest / the cheapest on the market.
Ends in -y (easy)It is the easiest company to work with.
Long (reliable, popular)It is the most reliable / the most popular of all.
Irregular (good, bad)It is the best / the worst option.
Regra de ourouse the + curto + est, ou the most + longo, nunca os dois: diga the fastest (não "the most fast") e the best (não "the goodest").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct superlative. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Our product is on the market."
"This device is of all."
"It is option for our customers."
"Our service is choice you can make."
"It is brand for pharmacies."
"It is solution for the customer."

Stage 2: Put the Recommendation in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?She compares it with the other options on the market.
?Gleice says which product is the best choice.
?She recommends it as the number one option.
?She gives the strongest reason, the most important advantage.
?She explains why it is the best solution for the customer.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I recommend this product to you.
Eu recomendo este produto para você.
It is the fastest on the market.
É o mais rápido do mercado.
It is the most reliable option of all.
É a opção mais confiável de todas.
It is the best choice and the clear winner.
É a melhor escolha e o vencedor claro.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You say your product is number one in speed. What do you say?
A "Our product is the most fast on the market."
B "Our product is the fastest on the market."
C "Our product is fastest on the market."
You say it is number one in trust. How do you say it?
A "It is the reliablest of all."
B "It is most reliable of all."
C "It is the most reliable of all."
You give your final recommendation. What do you say?
A "I recommend it. It is the best choice."
B "I recommend it. It is the goodest choice."
C "I recommend it. It is the most good choice."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Recommend your best product in five sentences. Name the product, give two superlatives, say your main advantage, and say why it is the best choice. Use superlatives: the fastest, the most reliable, the best.

Survival Card -- Lesson 14

1I recommend this product to you.Eu recomendo este produto para você.
2It is the fastest on the market.É o mais rápido do mercado.
3It is the most reliable option of all.É a opção mais confiável de todas.
4Our company is the market leader.Nossa empresa é a líder do mercado.
5It is the best choice and the clear winner.É a melhor escolha e o vencedor claro.
Aula 15 -- Pre-class

Sharing Good News -- We Have Done It (Present Perfect)

Announce results and achievements. New vocabulary: announce, result, goal, target, achieve, success, milestone, proud. Use the present perfect to share recent good news: have / has + past participle (we have launched, we have reached, the team has worked, we have done it). Use have with I, you, we, they and has with he, she, the team.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Announce -- to tell people some news in an official way
"I want to announce some good news." (anunciar)
Result -- what happens because of the team's work
"The results this year are excellent." (resultado)
Goal -- something you want to reach in your work
"We have achieved our main goal." (meta)
Target -- the exact number or result you want to reach
"We have reached our sales target." (alvo / meta numérica)
Achieve -- to reach a goal after hard work
"We have achieved a great result." (alcançar / conquistar)
Success -- when you reach your goal and do very well
"This year is a big success for us." (sucesso)
Milestone -- a big, important point that the team reaches
"We have reached a big milestone." (marco)
Proud -- very happy about something good you did
"I am very proud of our team." (orgulhoso)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Announce
Result
Goal
Target
Achieve
Success
Milestone
Proud

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Sofia wants to announce the good news of the year. The team has worked very hard, and they have reached the sales target. They have launched two new products and have achieved their main goal. The results are the best in five years, and they have reached a big milestone. This year is a great success, and Sofia is very proud of everyone.

1. What has the team done with the sales target?
A They have reached the sales target.
B They have lost the sales target.
C They will reach it next year.
2. Why are the results so good?
A Because the team has done nothing.
B Because the team has worked very hard.
C Because they are the cheapest.
3. How does Sofia feel about the year?
A She is worried about the team.
