B She is sad about the results.
C She is very proud of everyone.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect (have / has + past participle)

GRAMMAR

Para anunciar um resultado recente (uma boa notícia verdadeira agora), use have / has + partícipio passado. Com I, you, we, they use have (we have launched, we have reached). Com he, she, the team use has (she has achieved, the team has worked). Verbos regulares: o partícipio termina em -ed (launch → launched, reach → reached, achieve → achieved). Verbos irregulares: do → done, win → won, grow → grown. Negativo: we have not reached. Pergunta: have we reached the target? Nunca esqueça o have / has e nunca diga "we have do". / To announce a recent result, use have / has + past participle. Use have with I, you, we, they and has with he, she, the team. Regular verbs add -ed; some verbs are irregular (done, won, grown).

Subject / typePresent perfect
I / you / we / theyWe have launched two new products.
he / she / the teamThe team has reached the sales target.
Irregular verbsWe have done it. We have won an award.
Negative / questionWe have not reached it yet. / Have we achieved it?
Regra de ourosempre use have ou has antes do verbo, no partícipio: diga we have reached (não "we reached") e we have done (não "we have do").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct present perfect. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"We our sales target."
"We two new products."
"The team very hard."
"She her main goal."
"We it together."
"We a big milestone."

Stage 2: Put the Announcement in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?She gives another achievement: they have launched two new products.
?Gleice says she has good news to share.
?She says she is very proud and thanks the team for the success.
?She announces the main result: the team has reached the sales target.
?She shares the milestone: they have signed a big partnership.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I want to announce some good news.
Eu quero anunciar uma boa notícia.
We have reached our sales target.
Nós alcançamos nossa meta de vendas.
The team has worked very hard.
A equipe trabalhou muito.
We have done it together, and I am very proud.
Nós conseguimos juntos, e estou muito orgulhosa.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You announce that the team reached the sales target. What do you say?
A "We reached our sales target this year."
B "We have reached our sales target this year."
C "We have reach our sales target this year."
You talk about the team's hard work. How do you say it?
A "The team have worked very hard."
B "The team has worked very hard."
C "The team has work very hard."
You give your final good news. What do you say?
A "We have done it together. I am very proud."
B "We have do it together. I am very proud."
C "We did it together, and the team have it."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Announce your team's good news in five sentences. Give two results, one milestone, and say why you are proud. Use the present perfect: we have reached, we have launched, the team has worked.

Survival Card -- Lesson 15

1I want to announce some good news.Eu quero anunciar uma boa notícia.
2We have reached our sales target.Nós alcançamos nossa meta de vendas.
3We have launched two new products.Nós lançamos dois produtos novos.
4The team has worked very hard.A equipe trabalhou muito.
5We have done it together, and I am very proud.Nós conseguimos juntos, e estou muito orgulhosa.
Aula 16 -- Pre-class

Looking Back, Looking Ahead -- Checkpoint (aulas 11 to 15)

A checkpoint of the whole business block, all together: your calls and emails happening now in the present continuous (I am calling a customer), your future plans with going to (we are going to launch a service), comparing and recommending products with comparatives and superlatives (it is more reliable than the old one; it is the most popular choice), and sharing results with the present perfect (we have reached our target). Review words: pitch, customer, compare, reliable, recommend, advantage, achieve, milestone. No new grammar -- we make what you learned strong.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Review the eight key words from the business block. Tap Listen to hear each one.

Pitch -- a short talk to present and sell an idea
"I gave a short pitch to the customer." (apresentação de venda)
Customer -- a person or company who buys from you
"I am calling a new customer." (cliente)
Compare -- to look at two things to see the difference
"I compare our product with the old one." (comparar)
Reliable -- it always works well; you can trust it
"Our product is the most reliable one." (confiável)
Recommend -- to say something is the best choice for someone
"I recommend our new product." (recomendar)
Advantage -- a good point that makes one option better
"The main advantage is the price." (vantagem)
Achieve -- to reach a goal you worked hard for
"We have achieved our main goal." (alcançar)
Milestone -- a big, important result you reach
"We have reached a big milestone." (marco)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Pitch
Customer
Compare
Reliable
Recommend
Advantage
Achieve
Milestone

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Right now, the team is calling new customers and is sending more emails. Next quarter, they are going to launch a new service. Their product is faster and more reliable than the old one, and many people say it is the most popular choice on the market. This year, the team has reached its sales target and has achieved a big milestone. They recommend the product to every customer.

1. What is the team doing right now?
A They are calling new customers and sending emails.
B They called customers last year.
C They are going to call customers.
2. How is their product, compared to the old one?
A It is faster then the old one.
B It is faster and more reliable than the old one.
C It is the faster of the market.
3. What has the team achieved this year?
A "The team have reach a milestone."
B "The team is achieving a milestone now."
C It has reached its target and achieved a big milestone.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Business Toolbox

GRAMMAR

Revisão do bloco. Present continuous: am/is/are + verbo-ing para o que acontece agora (I am calling). Going to: am/is/are going to + verbo na base para planos futuros (we are going to launch). Comparativo: adjetivo curto + -er + than, ou more + adjetivo longo + than (faster than, more reliable than) -- sempre THAN, nunca "then". Superlativo: the + adjetivo + -est, ou the most + adjetivo longo (the cheapest, the most popular). Present perfect: have/has + particio para resultados (we have reached). / Block review. Present continuous for now; going to for future plans; comparatives (with than) to compare two; superlatives (with the) for the best; present perfect (have/has + participle) for results.

ToolExample
Now -- present continuousI am calling a customer now.
Future plan -- going toWe are going to launch a service.
Compare two -- comparative (+ than)It is more reliable than the old one.
The best -- superlative (the ...)It is the most popular choice.
Result -- present perfectWe have reached our target.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct form. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Right now, I a new customer."
"Next month, we a new service."
"Our product is the old one."
"It is product on the market."
"This year, we our sales target."
"We a big milestone."

Stage 2: Put the Review in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the business review in the correct order.

?They compare the product: it is faster and more reliable than the old one.
?The team is calling customers right now.
?They share the results: they have reached the target and signed two partnerships.
?They make a future plan: next quarter they are going to launch a service.
?They recommend it: it is the most popular choice in the region.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Right now, I am calling a new customer.
Agora mesmo, estou ligando para um novo cliente.
Next month, we are going to launch a new service.
No próximo mês, vamos lançar um novo serviço.
Our product is more reliable than the old one.
Nosso produto é mais confiável que o antigo.
This year, we have reached our sales target.
Este ano, atingimos nossa meta de vendas.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You say what you are doing at this moment. What do you say?
A "I calling a customer now."
B "I am calling a customer right now."
C "I am going to call a customer now."
You compare your product with another one. What do you say?
A "It is more reliable then the old one."
B "It is more reliable than the old one."
C "It is the more reliable of two."
You share a result at the end of the year. What do you say?
A "We have reach our target."
B "We are reaching our target now."
C "We have reached our target this year."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Run a short business review in five sentences. Say what your team is doing now (present continuous), one future plan (going to), how your product compares (comparative), why it is the best (superlative), and one result this year (present perfect).

Survival Card -- Lesson 16

1Right now, I am calling a customer.Agora mesmo, estou ligando para um cliente.
2Next month, we are going to launch a service.No próximo mês, vamos lançar um serviço.
3It is more reliable than the old one.É mais confiável que o antigo.
4It is the best choice, and I recommend it.É a melhor escolha, e eu a recomendo.
5This year, we have reached our target.Este ano, atingimos nossa meta.
Aula 17 -- Pre-class

Talking About Experiences -- present perfect & past simple

A new block: talk about your experiences with the present perfect (have/has + past participle) when you do NOT say exactly when -- I have visited many clients, I have travelled abroad. Use ever in questions (Have you ever travelled abroad?), never for no times, already and yet. Then contrast it with the past simple for a finished moment with a time word -- last year, I went to a conference in London. New words: experience, abroad, client, conference, visit, negotiate, trip, recently.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Learn the eight new words about experiences, travel, and work. Tap Listen to hear each one.

Experience -- something you have done or lived through
"I have a lot of work experience." (experiência)
Abroad -- in or to another country
"I have travelled abroad many times." (no exterior)
Client -- a company or person you work for or sell to
"I have visited many clients this year." (cliente)
Conference -- a big work event with talks and people
"I went to a conference in London." (conferência)
Visit -- to go and see a person, client, or place
"I have visited clients in five countries." (visitar)
Negotiate -- to talk with someone to agree on a deal
"I have negotiated many contracts." (negociar)
Trip -- a short journey to a place for work or fun
"I have learned a lot from each trip." (viagem)
Recently -- a short time ago; not long ago
"Recently, I have started a new project." (recentemente)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Experience
Abroad
Client
Conference
Visit
Negotiate
Trip
Recently

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

I have visited many clients and I have travelled abroad several times. Have you ever been to a conference abroad? Last year, I went to a conference in London and I presented our product. I have negotiated many contracts, but I have never missed an important meeting. Recently, I have started a new project, and it is a great experience.

1. What experiences has the speaker had?
A They have visited many clients and travelled abroad.
B They are visiting a client right now.
C They are going to visit a client.
2. What did the speaker do last year?
A They have gone to London last year.
B They went to a conference in London and presented a product.
C They will go to London.
3. Has the speaker ever missed an important meeting?
A "They have missed many meetings."
B "They missed a meeting last year."
C No, they have never missed an important meeting.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect & Past Simple

GRAMMAR

Present perfect: have/has + particio (visited, gone, been) para EXPERIENCIAS, quando NAO dizemos exatamente quando -- I have visited many clients. Use ever em perguntas (Have you ever..?), never para nenhuma vez, already (ja) e yet (ainda). Past simple: verbo + -ed ou irregular (went, met, presented) para um MOMENTO TERMINADO com palavra de tempo (yesterday, last year) -- Last year, I went to London. / Present perfect for experiences (no exact time, with ever/never/already/yet); past simple for a finished moment with a time word.

TenseExample
Experience -- present perfectI have visited many clients.
Question -- everHave you ever travelled abroad?
Not once -- neverI have never worked abroad.
Already / not yetI have already visited Paris. I have not visited Rome yet.
Finished moment -- past simpleLast year, I went to London.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct form. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"I many clients abroad."
"Last year, I to a conference in London."
"Have you a big contract?"
"I abroad for work."
"Yesterday, I a new client."
"Recently, we a new project."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put David's experiences in the correct order.

?He uses ever and never: he has never worked with a difficult client.
?He shares an experience with the present perfect: he has visited many clients.
?He asks about your experiences: what work experiences have you had?
?He gives a finished moment with the past simple: last year, he went to London.
?He talks about a recent experience: recently, he has started a new project.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I have visited many clients abroad.
Eu visitei muitos clientes no exterior.
Have you ever travelled abroad for work?
Você já viajou ao exterior a trabalho?
Last year, I went to a conference in London.
No ano passado, fui a uma conferência em Londres.
Recently, I have started a new project.
Recentemente, comecei um novo projeto.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You talk about an experience, without saying when. What do you say?
A "I visited many clients." (no time)
B "I have visited many clients."
C "I have went to many clients."
You talk about a finished moment last year. What do you say?
A "Last year, I have gone to London."
B "Last year, I went to London."
C "Last year, I go to London."
You ask about someone's experience. What do you say?
A "Did you ever travelled abroad?"
B "Have you ever travel abroad?"
C "Have you ever travelled abroad?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Tell your career story in five sentences. Share three experiences with the present perfect (something you have visited, negotiated, or learned), one finished moment in the past simple (a trip or conference last year), and what you have done recently.

Survival Card -- Lesson 17

1I have visited many clients abroad.Eu visitei muitos clientes no exterior.
2Have you ever travelled abroad for work?Você já viajou ao exterior a trabalho?
3I have never worked abroad.Eu nunca trabalhei no exterior.
4Last year, I went to a conference in London.No ano passado, fui a uma conferência em Londres.
5Recently, I have started a new project.Recentemente, comecei um novo projeto.
Aula 18 -- Pre-class

Talking About Your Career Story -- present perfect with for & since

Tell your career story and say HOW LONG you have done something. Use the present perfect (have/has + past participle) with FOR + a period of time (I have worked here for twelve years) and SINCE + a start point (I have been a manager since 2018). Ask "How long have you...?" New words: position, hire, skill, background, field, senior, loyal, dedicated.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Learn the eight new words about your career and job history. Tap Listen to hear each one.

Position -- your job or role in a company
"I have held this position since 2018." (cargo)
Hire -- to give someone a job in a company
"The company hired me in 2012." (contratar)
Skill -- something you can do well, learned with practice
"I have built a strong skill in sales." (habilidade)
Background -- your past study, training, and work history
"My background is in chemistry." (formação)
Field -- your area of work or study
"I have worked in this field for fifteen years." (área)
Senior -- experienced, with a high-level role
"I have been a senior manager since 2018." (sênior)
Loyal -- faithful; someone who stays with the company
"I have stayed loyal to my team for years." (leal)
Dedicated -- working very hard and caring about your job
"She has been dedicated to her team for years." (dedicado)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Position
Hire
Skill
Background
Field
Senior
Loyal
Dedicated

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

I have worked at this company for twelve years, and I have been a senior manager since 2018. My background is in chemistry, and my field is commercial strategy. The company hired me in 2012 for a junior position, and I have built a strong skill every year. I have stayed loyal and dedicated to my team for a long time. How long have you worked in your field?

1. How long has the speaker worked at the company?
A They have worked there for twelve years.
B They worked there last year.
C They are going to work there.
2. Since when have they been a senior manager?
A They have been a manager for 2018.
B They have been a senior manager since 2018.
C They will be a manager in 2018.
3. What is the speaker's background?
A "Their background is for chemistry."
B "They have hired chemistry."
C Their background is in chemistry, in the field of commercial strategy.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- For & Since

GRAMMAR

Para dizer HA QUANTO TEMPO, use o present perfect (have/has + particio) com FOR ou SINCE. FOR + um periodo de tempo (for five years, for two months) -- I have worked here for twelve years. SINCE + um ponto de inicio (since 2015, since March, since I joined) -- I have been a manager since 2018. Pergunta: How long have you + particio? -- How long have you worked at Sanofi? NUNCA "since five years" nem "for 2015". / Present perfect + FOR (a period) or SINCE (a start point) to say how long.

WordExample
FOR + a period of timeI have worked here for twelve years.
SINCE + a start point (year)I have been a manager since 2018.
SINCE + a start point (month)I have lived here since March.
FOR + a short periodI have known him for two months.
Question -- how longHow long have you worked at Sanofi?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"I have worked here twelve years."
"I have been a manager 2018."
"She has lived in São Paulo 2010."
"We have known each other two months."
"How long in this field?"
"I have been dedicated to my team years."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put David's career story in the correct order.

?He describes his background and field: my background is in chemistry, and my field is commercial strategy.
?He gives a duration with for: I have worked here for twelve years.
?He asks about your career: how long have you worked in your field?
?He gives a start point with since: I have been a senior manager since 2018.
?He talks about his skills and loyalty: I have learned a new skill, and I have stayed loyal.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I have worked here for twelve years.
Eu trabalho aqui há doze anos.
I have been a senior manager since 2018.
Sou gerente sênior desde 2018.
My background is in chemistry.
Minha formação é em química.
How long have you worked at Sanofi?
Há quanto tempo você trabalha na Sanofi?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You say how long you have done your job, using a period of time. What do you say?
A "I have worked here since five years."
B "I have worked here for five years."
C "I work here for five years."
You say since when you have been in your position, using a start point. What do you say?
A "I have been a manager for 2018."
B "I have been a manager since 2018."
C "I am a manager since 2018."
You ask a colleague how long they have done something. What do you say?
A "How long do you work here?"
B "Since how long you work here?"
C "How long have you worked here?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Tell your career story in five sentences. Say how long you have worked at Sanofi (for + a period), since when you have been in your position (since + a start point), your background and field, a skill you have built, and why you have stayed loyal and dedicated.

Survival Card -- Lesson 18

1I have worked here for twelve years.Eu trabalho aqui há doze anos.
2I have been a senior manager since 2018.Sou gerente sênior desde 2018.
3My background is in chemistry.Minha formação é em química.
4How long have you worked at Sanofi?Há quanto tempo você trabalha na Sanofi?
5I have been dedicated to my team for years.Sou dedicada à minha equipe há anos.
Aula 19 -- Pre-class

Job Interviews -- answering "Tell me about yourself"

Be ready for a job interview in English. Answer the big question -- "Tell me about yourself" -- using the present perfect you already know (I have worked, I have managed). Talk about your strengths and weaknesses, stay confident and honest, and answer questions about your qualification and salary. New words: interview, candidate, strength, weakness, confident, honest, qualification, salary.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Learn the eight new words about job interviews. Tap Listen to hear each one.

Interview -- a meeting where a company asks you questions about a job
"I have an interview on Monday." (entrevista)
Candidate -- a person who wants the job and goes to the interview
"I am the right candidate for this team." (candidato)
Strength -- something you are very good at
"My biggest strength is communication." (ponto forte)
Weakness -- something you are working to improve
"One weakness is that I am too dedicated." (ponto fraco)
Confident -- sure of yourself; calm and positive
"Stay calm and confident in the interview." (confiante)
Honest -- telling the truth; not lying
"Be honest about your strengths." (honesto)
Qualification -- the study, training, or experience that makes you right for a job
"I have a good qualification for this role." (qualificação)
Salary -- the money a company pays you for your work
"My salary expectation is fair and open." (salário)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Interview
Candidate
Strength
Weakness
Confident
Honest
Qualification
Salary

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the interview answer and answer the questions.

"Thank you for this interview. Let me tell you about myself. My background is in biology, and I have worked in the pharmaceutical field for ten years. In my last position, I have managed a large sales team. My biggest strength is communication, and I am a very confident and honest person. One weakness is that I am too dedicated. I have a good qualification for this role, and I really want this job. I believe I am the right candidate for your team."

1. How does the candidate answer "Tell me about yourself"?
A She gives her background and her experience with the present perfect.
B She asks the interviewer about the salary.
C She says nothing about her work.
2. What is the candidate's strength?
A Her strength is that she is too dedicated.
B Her biggest strength is communication.
C She has no strength.
3. How does she sound in the interview?
A She is afraid and unsure.
B She is not honest.
C She is confident and honest.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Answering "Tell me about yourself"

GRAMMAR

Numa entrevista, a pergunta mais importante é "Tell me about yourself" (Fale sobre você). Use o present perfect que você já conhece (have/has + particípio) para falar da sua experiência: I have worked in this field for ten years. I have managed a sales team. Fale do seu ponto forte (My biggest strength is...) e de um ponto fraco (One weakness is...). Termine com confiança: I am the right candidate for this job. NUNCA responda só com "I am Gleice" -- conte sua história profissional. / Use the present perfect to answer interview questions about your experience, then share a strength, a weakness, and why you are the right candidate.

QuestionStrong answer
Tell me about yourself.My background is in biology, and I have worked in this field for ten years.
What is your biggest strength?My biggest strength is communication.
What is one weakness?One weakness is that I am too dedicated.
Why do you want this job?I am the right candidate, and I really want this job.
Stay calmBe honest and confident. Take your time.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Thank you for this ."
"My biggest is communication."
"One is that I am too dedicated."
"Please be about your strengths."
"I am the right for this team."
"My expectation is fair and open."

Stage 2: Put the Interview in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the interview in the correct order.

?He asks about a strength and a weakness, and the candidate is honest.
?The interviewer welcomes the candidate and says thank you for coming.
?Finally, he asks about salary expectations and says she is a strong candidate.
?He asks the first question: tell me about yourself and your background.
?He asks why she wants the job and about her qualification.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Let me tell you about myself.
Deixe-me falar sobre mim.
My biggest strength is communication.
Meu maior ponto forte é a comunicação.
I am a confident and honest person.
Sou uma pessoa confiante e honesta.
I am the right candidate for this job.
Sou a candidata certa para este emprego.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

The interviewer says "Tell me about yourself." What is the best answer?
A "I am Gleice. That is all."
B "My background is in chemistry, and I have worked in this field for ten years."
C "What is the salary?"
The interviewer asks about a weakness. What is the best answer?
A "I have no weakness at all."
B "One weakness is that I am too dedicated, but I am working on it."
C "I do not want to answer."
You want to show you are the best person for the role. What do you say?
A "Maybe I am okay for this job."
B "I am not sure I can do it."
C "I am the right candidate, and I have a good qualification for this role."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

You are in a job interview. Answer "Tell me about yourself" in five sentences. Give your background, say how long you have worked in your field (present perfect), name one strength, name one weakness, and say why you are the right candidate. Stay confident and honest.

Survival Card -- Lesson 19

1Let me tell you about myself.Deixe-me falar sobre mim.
2My biggest strength is communication.Meu maior ponto forte é a comunicação.
3One weakness is that I am too dedicated.Um ponto fraco é que sou dedicada demais.
4I am a confident and honest person.Sou uma pessoa confiante e honesta.
5I am the right candidate for this job.Sou a candidata certa para este emprego.
Aula 20 -- Pre-class

Checkpoint 4 -- The Full Story (aulas 17 to 19)

A checkpoint of the whole Experiences block, all together: life experiences with the present perfect (I have visited many clients), finished moments with the past simple (I started here in 2012), a duration with for and a start point with since (I have worked here for ten years; I have been a manager since 2018), and a job interview answer (tell me about yourself; my biggest strength is communication). Review words: experience, background, field, achievement, interview, candidate, strength, confident. No new grammar -- we make what you learned strong.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Review the eight key words from the Experiences block. Tap Listen to hear each one.

Experience -- something you have done or lived in your life
"I have a lot of experience in sales." (experiência)
Background -- your studies and your past work history
"My background is in chemistry." (formação)
Field -- your area of work or study
"I have worked in this field for ten years." (área de atuação)
Achievement -- a big thing you have reached and are proud of
"My biggest achievement is my last project." (conquista)
Interview -- a meeting where you answer questions for a job
"I have an interview on Monday." (entrevista)
Candidate -- a person who wants and applies for the job
"I am a strong candidate for this role." (candidato)
Strength -- something you are very good at
"My biggest strength is communication." (ponto forte)
Confident -- feeling sure and calm about yourself
"In the interview, I was calm and confident." (confiante)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Experience
Background
Field
Achievement
Interview
Candidate
Strength
Confident

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Gleice has worked in the commercial field for many years and has visited many clients. She started her career as a junior and joined this company in 2012. She has been a manager since 2018, and her background is in business. In her last interview, she was calm, honest, and confident, and the company said she was a strong candidate. Her biggest strength is communication.

1. How long has Gleice worked in the commercial field?
A She has worked in the field for many years.
B She works in the field next year.
C She is going to work in the field.
2. When did she join this company?
A She has joined this company in 2012.
B She joined this company in 2012.
C She joins this company in 2012.
3. Since when has she been a manager?
A She has been a manager for 2018.
B She was a manager since 2018.
C She has been a manager since 2018.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Experiences Toolbox

GRAMMAR

Revisão do bloco. Present perfect: have/has + particio para experiências da vida sem tempo definido (I have visited). Past simple: verbo no passado para um momento terminado, com data (I started in 2012). For: duração, um período de tempo (for ten years). Since: ponto de início (since 2018) -- nunca "for 2018" nem "since ten years". / Block review. Present perfect for a life experience with no time; past simple for a finished moment with a date; for for a duration; since for a start point.

ToolExample
Life experience, no time -- present perfectI have visited many clients.
Finished moment, with a date -- past simpleI started this job in 2012.
Duration -- for + a periodI have worked here for ten years.
Start point -- since + a point in timeI have been a manager since 2018.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct form. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"I many clients in my career."
"I this job in 2012."
"I have worked in this field ten years."
"I have been a manager 2018."
"My biggest is communication."
"I am a strong for this role."

Stage 2: Put the Story in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the career story in the correct order.

?A duration with for: I have worked here for ten years.
?A life experience with the present perfect: I have visited many countries.
?An interview answer: my biggest strength is communication, and I am a strong candidate.
?A finished moment with the past simple: I started this job in 2012.
?A start point with since: I have been a manager since 2018.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I have visited many clients in my career.
Eu visitei muitos clientes na minha carreira.
I started this job in 2012.
Eu comecei neste emprego em 2012.
I have worked in this field for ten years.
Eu trabalho nesta área há dez anos.
My biggest strength is communication.
Meu maior ponto forte é a comunicação.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You talk about a life experience with no time. What do you say?
A "I visit many clients in my career."
B "I have visited many clients in my career."
C "I am visiting many clients in my career."
You give a finished moment with a year. What do you say?
A "I have started this job in 2012."
B "I started this job in 2012."
C "I start this job in 2012."
You give a start point in the interview. What do you say?
A "I have been a manager for 2018."
B "I have been a manager since ten years."
C "I have been a manager since 2018."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Answer a job interview in five sentences. Say a life experience (present perfect), when you started (past simple), how long you have worked here (for), since when you have been a manager (since), and your biggest strength (interview).

Survival Card -- Lesson 20

1I have visited many clients in my career.Eu visitei muitos clientes na minha carreira.
2I started this job in 2012.Eu comecei neste emprego em 2012.
3I have worked in this field for ten years.Eu trabalho nesta área há dez anos.
4I have been a manager since 2018.Eu sou gerente desde 2018.
5My biggest strength is communication, and I am a strong candidate.Meu maior ponto forte é a comunicação, e sou uma ótima candidata.

IN CLASS -- Select a Lesson

09
Meetings at Sanofi

10
Block 2 Review
Progress Check: Past, Present & Meetings -- 27 slides
11

12

13

14
The Best Choice

15
Sharing Good News

16

Checkpoint 3: Business Communication Review -- 27 slides
17

18

19
